name: Seville Orange Marmalade
recipeInstructions:
(Makes 6 pounds)
1. Measure the water into a preserving pan.
2. Cut the lemon and oranges in half and squeeze out the juice.
3. Add the juice to the water; place pips and bits of pith on muslin.
4. Cut orange peel into quarters, then thin shreds.
5. Add shreds to water with any pips or spare pith on muslin.
6. Tie pips loosely in muslin to form a bag; tie this to pan handle.
7. Bring liquid to simmering point and gently simmer for 2 hours until peel is soft.
8. Test setting by pressing between finger and thumb; if skin crinkles, it's set.
9. Remove pip bag, let cool on saucer.
10. Add sugar to pan, stir over low heat until crystals dissolve completely.
11. Increase heat to high; squeeze pip bag into pan to extract pectin jelly-like substance.
12. Stir or whisk mixture thoroughly.
13. When mixture boils hard, start timer; test with cold plate after 15 minutes by pushing with finger.
14. Continue boiling and testing at 10-minute intervals until set.
15. Remove from heat (dispense scum with half a teaspoon butter); let settle for 20 minutes.
16. Heat jars in moderate oven for 5 minutes; pour marmalade into jars, cover with waxed discs, seal while hot.
17. Label when jars are cold.
